items.title: Der häufigste Fehler bei Produkt-Zielen Wie du mit KI bessere Produkt-Ziele formulierst, die nicht nur Features beschreiben, sondern die Unternehmensstrategie umsetzen
items.excerpt: Betrachte diese Produktziele. Was fällt dir auf? „Wir bauen ein KI-gestütztes Help-Center.“ „Wir integrieren eine Chatbot-Funktion.“ „Wir erstellen ein neues Onboarding.“ Sie klingen konkret. Sie beschreiben Fortschritt. Sie klingen nach Erfolg. Allerdings verbergen sie ein Problem: Erinnere dich an Microsoft Office 97. Im Jahr 1997 erschien der Office-Assistent „Karl Klammer“. Wenn du dein Dokument mit „Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren“ begonnen hast, tauchte am unteren Bildschirmrand eine animierte Sprechblase auf. Sie hatte die Form einer Büroklammer mit Augen und bot dir ihre Hilfe an: „Ansch
items.titleJa: プロダクト目標の最大の落とし穴 AIで戦略を実現する目標設定
items.summaryJa: 多くのプロダクト目標は機能の追加を述べるだけで、真の事業戦略と結びついていない。AIを活用した目標設定では、単なる機能実装ではなく、企業全体の戦略実現に貢献する目標の定義が重要であり、その違いがプロダクトの成功を左右する。

items.title: Explore vs. Exploit – ein kleines Spiel über zwei fundamentale Arbeitsmodi
items.excerpt: Das Explore-vs-Exploit-Game Teilnehmer Das Spiel funktioniert gut mit 1 bis 8 Spielern pro Team. Bei größeren Gruppen bietet es sich an, mehrere Teams parallel spielen zu lassen. Gerade bei mehreren Teams entstehen interessante Dynamiken, weil die Teams anfangen, sich gegenseitig zu beobachten und ihre aktuelle Vorgehensweise hinterfragen, wenn sie im Rückstand sind. Jedes Team benötigt zusätzlich eine Person in der Rolle des Regelwächters. Diese Person kennt die geheimen Regeln des Spiels und führt nach jedem Zug bestimmte Aktionen aus. Teilnehmer können auch als Beobachter teilnehmen. Die Ro
items.titleJa: 探索 vs. 活用 - 2つの根本的な働き方を学ぶゲーム
items.summaryJa: チームワークと意思決定スキルを高める協力ゲーム。1〜8人のチームで参加でき、複数チーム参加時はチーム間の相互観察から学習効果が高まる。秘密ルールを管理するファシリテーターが必要で、参加者は観察者としても学習できる。

items.title: Brain Sensei vs PM PrepCast
items.excerpt: I’ve long used products from Cornelius Fichtner of The Project Management PrepCast fame for my own professional development. But I also know the team behind Brain Sensei. So how does Brain Sensei compare to the PM PrepCast? That’s what we’re covering in this article. I’ve used them both, I’ve had conversations with both teams, and over the year’s I’ve come to appreciate what they are both good at. Let’s do the comparison of their PMP training! Packages For a start, both companies offer Project Management Professional (PMP)® training. Brain Sensei offers: A self-paced PMP prep course that inclu
items.titleJa: Brain Sensei vs PM PrepCast: PMP資格取得教材の徹底比較
items.summaryJa: PMP(プロジェクトマネジメントプロフェッショナル)資格取得向けの学習教材「Brain Sensei」と「PM PrepCast」を実際に使用した著者が両社の特徴、パッケージ内容を比較。プロジェクトマネジメント専門家として両チームとの関係がある執筆者が、各教材の強みを詳細に解説しています。

items.title: 🎙️ How I AI: GPT-5.6 review, How a solo builder runs 24/7 local AI, and What an agent harness is and how to build one

items.title: Spec-Driven Development Isn't Waterfall Unless You're Using It That Way
items.excerpt: Originally published on yuvalyeret.com. Last summer, while I was vibe-coding my own scorecard app, I kept running into dead ends. Not dramatic ones — the annoying kind. I would get something working, then realize the path was wrong. Or the behavior was close but not quite right. Or I had built a capability that looked useful in isolation but did not actually help the overall product move forward. So I started thinking more up front. I used plan mode more. And eventually I started using spec-driven development more formally. Which raises the obvious question: did I just give up agility and go b
items.titleJa: 仕様駆動開発はウォーターフォールではない
items.summaryJa: Spec-Driven Developmentを導入した開発者の実例を紹介。当初のアジャイル開発では要件の不明確さから手戻りが多発したため、計画段階での仕様定義をより厳密に行うことで、プロダクト全体の方向性を確認しながら開発を進める効果を解説しています。

items.title: Scrum Kumbaya
items.excerpt: Image I was speaking with a Scrum team member recently whose Scrum Master was trying just a little too hard. The Daily Scrum had turned into a positivity meeting rather than a quick, collaborative touch base. Every day it stretched to 30 minutes (!), with everyone asked to share a thought of the day and so on. The intention was good — but the impact was... just a bit much too much time in meetings. Team building is great, but when it happens every day, it can be a little overwhelming. As one person put it, "This much positivity in the morning is a lot to take!" The Purpose of the Daily Scrum W
items.titleJa: デイリースクラムにおけるポジティビティの過剰さへの警告
items.summaryJa: スクラムマスターが意図的な「チームビルディング」を重視するあまり、デイリースクラムが30分に延長され、参加者負担が増加した事例を紹介。デイリースクラムの本来の目的と、効率的な協調確認に焦点を当てることの重要性について論じています。

items.title: This solo builder runs 24/7 local AI on his own hardware | Alex Finn
items.excerpt: Watch now | 🎙️ Alex Finn breaks down his five-computer local AI setup, the Claude Code build-and-review loop that ships features while he sleeps, and why unlimited local intelligence beats a $20 subscription

items.summaryJa: Alex Finnが5台のコンピュータで構築したローカルAI環境と、Claude Codeを使ったビルド・レビュー自動化ループについて解説。月額$20のサブスクリプション不要で、無制限のローカルAI処理により、開発者が睡眠中も機能をデリバリーし続ける仕組みを紹介しています。

items.title: [Free Tutorial] Master Building AI Agents
items.excerpt: In this blog, I teach you how to build AI Agents using two powerful examples: 1- An AI Agent to collect user stories from supporting tickets (Video Tutorial) 2- An AI Agent to write release notes (Text Tutorial) When you learn the principles and main process, you can build whatever AI Agents you want. So, stay with me. 1- The AI Agent to collect user stories from supporting tickets 2- The AI Agent to write release notes Image Writing release notes is a repetitive task for Product Owners. Wouldn't it be great if we could delegate it to an AI Agent? Let’s take a look at how we can do just that.
items.titleJa: AIエージェント構築のマスターガイド

items.titleJa: プロダクトマネージャーのリーダーシップへのキャリアパス

items.title: How to Create a Powerful PMO Mission Statement
items.excerpt: There's a lot riding on getting your PMO mission statement right. After all, the mission statement is what will guide the PMO as it strives to achieve its goals and objectives. Creating a powerful PMO mission statement doesn't have to be difficult - but it does require some thought and careful planning. Why have a mission statement for the PMO? The PMO is responsible for ensuring that an organization's projects are well-organized, well-run, and produce the desired results. A key part of a PMO leader's job is to develop and maintain project management standards and processes and to support the
items.titleJa: 強力なPMOミッションステートメントの策定方法

Join the Project Management Resource Library to get the template and lots of other free project management templates, checklists, and guides. Whether you want to understand how customers move through your small business, or mapping the processes used on a major civil engineering project, SIPOC is a visual process mapping tool that can help yo

items.title: Just Looking
items.excerpt: In Looking Good, Testers! I made the case that testing is about looking and that delegating the looking to someone or something else involves a risk calculation: do you both understand the task the same way, is the third party likely to carry out the task in the way you requested, how easily can you check any results they give back, could they complement you in some way, how will you keep your knowledge and skills up to date if you don't do this task any more, how much does this task matter, how much do you care about any of that, ...? I didn't say anything there about selecting what to look a
items.titleJa: テスター自身が確認することの重要性
items.summaryJa: テストの本質は「確認する」ことであり、その役割を他者やツールに委譲する際には、理解の一致、実行の正確性、結果検証の容易性など複数のリスク要因を検討する必要があります。自動化やアウトソースの判断には、知識・スキルの維持や業務の重要度など多角的な視点が求められます。
